incorrects.description: Student selected Scale of 1 - would require too many axis marks for values up to 70

steps.text: That would need a lot of lines on the axis. All the values end in 0 or 5. Which bigger scale would be easier to read?

incorrects.description: Student selected Scale of 2 - would require too many axis marks for values up to 70

steps.text: That would need a lot of lines on the axis. All the values end in 0 or 5. Which bigger scale would be easier to read?

incorrects.description: Student selected Scale of 5 - works but not most efficient given maximum value 70

steps.text: That works! But notice the biggest value is 70. Scale of 10 would give you fewer lines and still show everything exactly.

incorrects.description: Student placed Red Team bar at incorrect height on first attempt

steps.text: 25 is halfway between 20 and 30. Find that spot.

incorrects.description: Student placed Red Team bar at incorrect height on second attempt

steps.text: Look at the axis. There's 20, then 30. 25 goes exactly halfway between those two marks.

incorrects.description: Student placed Red Team bar at incorrect height three or more times. System models the correct placement.

params.description: System demonstrates placing Red Team bar at 25.

steps.text: I'll show you how to find 25. First I locate 20 on the axis, then 30. 25 is exactly halfway between those two tick marks. That's where the bar ends. The bar for Red Team shows 25 points.

incorrects.description: Student placed Blue Team bar at incorrect height on first attempt

steps.text: Look for 40 on the axis. It lands right on a tick mark.

incorrects.description: Student placed Blue Team bar at incorrect height on second attempt

steps.text: 40 is a tick mark on the axis. Find that line and place the bar there.

incorrects.description: Student placed Blue Team bar at incorrect height three or more times. System models the correct placement.

params.description: System demonstrates placing Blue Team bar at 40.

steps.text: Let me show you. 40 is one of the tick marks on the axis. I find 40 on the axis and extend the bar to that line. The bar ends right at 40. Blue Team scored 40 points.

steps.text: I'll show you how to find 55. First I locate 50 on the axis, then 60. 55 is exactly halfway between those two tick marks. That's where the bar ends. The bar for Green Team shows 55 points.
